FUTURE ACCOUNTING

Off Calendar Apr-20-2021 Off Calendar

ORDER OF STATUS HEARING
Off Calendar Apr-20-2021 Off Calendar

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status Of Ms. Ozonsi Obtaining Counsel, Or Removal Of Ms. Ozonsi As Trustee
Off Calendar Apr-20-2021 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Carolyn Ozonsi, via CourtCall, in propria persona.

The court advises Ms. Ozonsi that it intends to terminate the court's supervision of this Trust matter. Ms. Ozonsi reports that she understands the court's position. The court's supervision of this Trust is terminated on the court's own motion. The Status of Ms. Ozonsi obtaining counsel, or removal as Trustee and the order to appear are taken off calendar. The Petition RE: Accounting #3 scheduled for May 11, 2021, is also ordered off calendar.

Judge: Hon. Joseph Quinn; Clerk: Robert Woods; Bailiff: Deputy Tauscher. Not Reported.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status Of Ms. Ozonsi Obtaining Counsel, Or Removal Of Ms. Ozonsi As Trustee
Off Calendar Mar-09-2021 Continued To Apr-20-21 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Carolyn Osonzi appeared by telephone in Propria Persona.

The Court asked if there is any development as far as someone else stepping in as the Trustee. Ms. Ozonsi stated that she can do the work, that she contacted some Attorneys, but no one wants to take the case, that her Niece can't serve due to an injury and that her Niece no longer wants to serve. Ms. Ozonsi also stated that she can carry out the work of the Trustee, that she has had the accounting done, but it is not filed, that she did not know she had to put a face page on it and that she has had the accounting for two months, but has been waiting to have an Attorney. In addition, Ms. Ozonsi stated that she has not received the death certificate, that she sent out for it through a Niece in Atlanta a couple of days ago and that the Niece is going to get the death certificate for her. The Court noted that the Trust was essentially under Court supervision because the Aunt was under a conservatorship, that the Aunt has passed away and stated that it will issue an order to show cause as to why the Court should not terminate supervision over this trust. The Court also explained that if supervision is terminated, Ms. Ozonsi will only be accountable to the Beneficiaries, not the Court. The order to show cause will be set for April 20, 2021 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. Status on Ms. Ozonsi obtaining Counsel, or removal of Ms. Ozonsi is continued to April 20, 2021 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: K. Kabasares. Not reported.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status Of Ms. Ozonsi Obtaining Counsel, Or Removal Of Ms. Ozonsi As Trustee
Off Calendar Jan-12-2021 Continued To Mar-09-21 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Carolyn Ozonsi appeared by telephone in Propria Persona.

The Court noted that there have been several continuances and that the withdrawal of prior Counsel was stipulated to. Ms. Ozonsi stated that she spoke with Attorney Charles Dressler on the 7th, that Mr. Dressler referred her to his partner Dan Presher and that she will speak with Mr. Presher today so that he can guide her through what she has to do. Ms. Ozonsi also stated that shortly after her Aunt died, she was hospitalized and has been very ill since then, that she has spoken with the other three beneficiaries and that the Guardian (Octavia Holborough) will send her a death certificate. The Court informed Ms. Ozonsi that an accounting from the end of the last accounting period to the date of death is needed. Ms. Ozonsi stated that she has an accounting, but it needs to be corrected by the Accountant. The Court explained to Ms. Ozonsi that it should be a petition for distribution and that she needs to do a final accounting unless she can get the beneficiaries to waive the accounting. The Court also stated that it will continue this one more time at which time Ms. Ozonsi needs to appear with Counsel, or she will need have a third party with a firm plan to bring this trust to a close, and questions if Ms. Holborough is getting compensation. Ms. Ozonsi stated that Ms. Holborough collects the Aunt's social security which is about $1.400.00 and that she doubts that Ms. Holborough will be seeking fees from the trust. Status of Ms. Ozonsi obtaining Counsel, or removal of Ms. Ozonsi as Trustee is continued to March 9, 2021 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: K. Kabasares. Not reported.

ORDER OF STATUS HEARING
Off Calendar Oct-27-2020 Off Calendar

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #2, Report, Fees
Off Calendar Nov-05-2020 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Carolyn Ozonsi appeared by telehpone in Propria Persona.

Ms. Ozonsi stated that Ms. Lewis called her today at about five minutes to nine stating that her telephone was out of order. Ms. Ozonsi also stated that she fired Ms. Lewis, that she agrees to Ms. Lewis' withdrawal and that she waives notice of the Motion to Be Relieved as Counsel. In addition, Ms. Ozonsi stated that she brought the papers to the Court and put them in the box, that the papers were returned to her because she did not include a cover page and that she is doing the best she can. Furthermore, Ms. Ozonsi addressed the Court's inquiries as to the Conservator's fee request in the amount of $12,000.00, the residential facility fees, the $220.00 payment to Ms. Callejo, the $970.00 caregiver fee to Octavia Holborough, etc. The issue of Ms. Ozonsi not having an Attorney was also discussed. The Court informed Ms. Ozonsi that if this can't be resolved, it will order her removal. Ms. Ozonsi stated that her Niece would be willing to serve. The Court informed Ms. Ozonsi that her Niece would have to get an Attorney. The Motion to Be Relieved as Counsel is granted although no order has been provided. The Second Account and Report of Trustee, etc. is under submission. The order to appear is off calendar. Status of Ms. Ozonsi obtaining Counsel, or removal of Ms. Ozonsi as Trustee is set for January 12, 2021 at 9:00 a.m., in dept. 204.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: K. Kabasares. Not reported.

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Be Relieved As Counsel
Off Calendar Oct-27-2020 Text Ruling

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Be Relieved As Counsel
Off Calendar Oct-06-2020 Continued To Oct-27-20 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Carolyn Ozonsi, Trustee in pro per (appearing by telephone).

The Court notes the absence of Cora Lewis, Esq. An Order to Appear shall issue to Cora Lewis, Esq, to appear on October 27, 2020 at 9:00 a.m. in Dept. 204 to explain why she failed to appear at hearing on October 6, 2020, why no proof of service for her Motion to be relieved as counsel has been filed, and why she has not complied with prior instructions of the Court. The Court will also consider imposing sanctions at that time.

The Court next addresses the pending accounting petition, noting that this is the 10th hearing on the Petition and that there does not appear to be any significant resolution of pending issues. Ms. Ozonsi advises that she remains unable to find replacement legal counsel, that she does not have a working computer, and is unable to prepare responsive pleadings to the Examiner Notes in a manner suitable for filing with the Court. The Court instructs Ms. Ozonsi to do what she can, even if handwritten as opposed to typed, so that the Court will have something to properly review and consider.

The Court continues the matter to October 27, 2020 at 9:00 a.m. in Dept. 204. Ms. Ozonsi is instructed to file her supplemental pleadings on or before October 20, 2020 with courtesy copies to the Examiner on or before Oct. 20th as well.

Judge: Hon. Joseph M. Quinn; Clerk: R. Michael Diles; Reporter: Not Reported; Bailiff: Deputy Johnson. (D204)

Off Calendar Sep-22-2020 Continued To Oct-06-20 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Be Relieved As Counsel
Off Calendar Aug-18-2020 Continued To Sep-22-20 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Carolyn Ozonsi appeared by telephone in Propria Persona.
Cora Lewis (Former Attorney for Ms. Ozonsi) appeared by zoom.

Ms. Ozonsi spoke of Ms. Barksdale's condition, stated that new Counsel told her that she couldn't come to court and that she could not be her Attorney because she was going into the hospital, and that she tried to get a new Attorney, but couldn't get one. Ms. Ozonsi also stated that she called the NAACP to try and get an Attorney, that she has an opportunity to discuss the case with an Attorney in Oakland, that Ms. Lewis hadn't filed a substitution of Attorney until February 20th, that she kept trying to contact Ms. Lewis and that she got no response. In addition, Ms. Ozonsi stated that she has not seen Ms. Lewis' motions. Ms. Lewis stated that she did not file a proof of service, but she has one and that Ms. Ozonsi fired her. The matter was passed and re-called at the end of the calendar. The Court informed Ms. Ozonsi that once it receives the proof of service, it will grant the Motion to Be Relieved as Counsel, that she will be self-represented and will have to resolve the issues in the accounting, that she will have to read the Probate Examiner's notes and respond to the notes, and that she needs to continue finding Counsel. The Motion to Be Relieved as Counsel, and the Second Account and Report of Trustee, etc. are continued to September 22, 2020 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. The Court stated that if the accounting does not get resolved, Ms. Ozonsi will be removed as the Trustee.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: C. Cabasares. Not reported.

Off Calendar Mar-26-2020 Continued To Jun-29-20 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #2, REPORT, FEES
Off Calendar Mar-02-2020 Continued To Apr-06-20 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Carolyn Ozonsi in propria persona.

Ms. Ozonsi stated that she did secure an attorney name Jeanette Fischer-Kaudia, that the attorney could not come today, that the new attorney will be available in about 4 days, that there was a fire thing at her senior living center so she sent her declaration through overnight mail, and she tried to file that declaration downstairs today but she did not have a proof of service. The Court instructed Ms. Ozonsi to tell her new attorney to work with her old attorney to file a substitution of attorney.

The second account and report of trustee petition for settlement of account and trustee fees is continued to April 6, 2020 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. Status for motion to be relieved as counsel or filing of substitution of attorney and filing of declaration re: counsel search also set for April 6, 2020.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn. Clerk: S. Le. Deputy: K. Tauscher. Matter not reported.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #2, REPORT, FEES
Off Calendar Jan-27-2020 Continued To Mar-02-20 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Cora Lewis former Attorney for Carolyn Ozonsi.
Carolyn Ozonsi in Propria Persona.

Ms. Lewis stated that she was terminated Ms. Ozonsi last April, that Ms. Ozonsi was supposed to seek new Counsel and is still seeking Counsel, that she will be filing a motion to be relieved and that she did not filed her motion earlier because she was waiting for Ms. Ozonsi to get someone. Ms. Ozonsi stated that Ms. Lewis called her 5-days ago, that no Attorney will take her case and that she thinks it may be because there is no substitution of attorney on file. The Court noted that when someone is acting in a Fiduciary capacity, the law prefers that they have an Attorney. Ms. Ozonsi stated that she has been calling the Bar Association, that they don't want to take her case, that she has been calling Private Lawyers and that for some reason when they look up her case number, they don't want to take her case. The Court directed Ms. Lewis to file her Motion to Be Relieved as Counsel. The Second Account and Report of Trustee, etc. is continued to March 2, 2020 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. Ms. Ozonsi is to file a declaration by February 24, 2020 regarding what efforts she has made since April of 2019 in obtaining new Counsel and any contacts she has made with other Attorneys. The order to appear is off calendar.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: R. Anasse. Not reported.
